data.title: Hershey Unveils One Hershey Strategy at 2026 Investor Day
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The Hershey Company ( (HSY) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On March 31, 2026, The Hershey Company hosted its 2026 Investor Day at the New York Stock Exchange, where executives detailed a strategy to “Lead Next Generation Snacking” through an integrated “One Hershey” commercial model across sweet, salty and functional snacks. Management emphasized a five-year innovation pipeline focused on premium and better-for-you offerings, plans for a more automated and AI-enabled supply chain to boost productivity, and a clear roadmap to restore margins and deliver balanced top- and bottom-line growth.

The company reaffirmed its 2026 full-year guidance, projecting net sales growth of 4% to 5%, organic net sales growth of 2.5% to 3.5% and a sharp rebound in profitability, with reported EPS expected to rise 79% to 89% and adjusted EPS 30% to 35%, partly aided by its 2025 acquisition of LesserEvil. The outlook and strategic agenda underscore Hershey’s intent to sustain top-tier shareholder returns while solidifying its competitive position in the evolving global snacking landscape.

The most recent analyst rating on (HSY) stock is a Hold with a $200.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on The Hershey Company stock, see the HSY Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HSY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HSY is a Neutral.

HSY scores positively on strong technical momentum and resilient cash generation, but the overall rating is held back by a sharp 2025 margin/earnings reset and a high P/E (~51) that increases downside if recovery is slower than guidance. The earnings call supports the outlook with clear recovery targets for 2026, though cocoa and pricing elasticity remain key risks.

To see Spark’s full report on HSY stock, click here.

More about The Hershey Company

The Hershey Company, listed on the NYSE under ticker HSY, is a global confectionery and snacking manufacturer best known for its iconic chocolate and candy brands. The company is increasingly positioning itself as a diversified snacking player, expanding from traditional sweets into salty, premium, better-for-you and functional snack categories to capture growth across the broader snacking market.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: $43.28B

An announcement from Canagold Resources ( (TSE:CCM) ) is now available.

On March 31, 2026, Canagold Resources Ltd. filed a Form 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for the month of March 2026, furnishing its annual disclosure package for the year ended December 31, 2025. The submission included management’s discussion and analysis, consolidated financial statements, and the CEO and CFO certifications of those annual filings.

Chief executive officer Catalin Kilofliski certified that the 2025 annual filings were free of material misstatements and fairly presented the company’s financial condition, performance and cash flows. He also confirmed that Canagold’s disclosure controls and internal control over financial reporting were designed and evaluated using the COSO framework, with no reported material weaknesses in design, underscoring the company’s focus on governance and reporting reliability for investors.

data.description:

The latest update is out from Masonglory Limited ( (MSGY) ).

On March 31, 2026, Masonglory Limited filed a Form 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, furnishing unaudited interim condensed consolidated financial statements as of September 30, 2025 and for the six months ended September 30, 2025 and 2024. The submission also included management’s discussion and analysis and full Inline XBRL data, signaling a step-up in financial transparency and regulatory compliance for shareholders and prospective investors.

The interim figures show that between March 31 and September 30, 2025, total assets rose to $11.65 million from $6.55 million, driven by higher contract assets, prepayments and new machinery, while total liabilities edged down to $2.95 million. Shareholders’ equity more than doubled to $8.70 million, supported by a sharp increase in additional paid-in capital and modestly higher retained earnings, even as revenue and net income for the six-month period declined year-on-year, suggesting capital-structure strengthening despite softer profitability.

More about Masonglory Limited

Masonglory Limited is a Hong Kong-based foreign private issuer listed in the United States, reporting under Form 20-F. The company operates through various subsidiaries and prepares its financial statements in U.S. dollars, indicating an international investor focus, although its specific operating industry and primary products or services are not detailed in this filing.

data.title: Skeena Resources Launches US$750 Million Notes Offering to Reshape Eskay Creek Financing
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Skeena Resources ( (TSE:SKE) ).

On March 31, 2026, Skeena Gold & Silver announced plans to issue US$750 million of senior secured notes due 2031, guaranteed by subsidiaries tied to the Eskay Creek project and secured by first-priority liens on key assets and accounts. The notes are to be privately placed to qualified institutional buyers in the United States and to investors in Canada under prospectus exemptions.

Skeena plans to allocate about US$184 million of the proceeds to partially buy back its existing US$200 million gold stream, cutting the stream percentage on Eskay Creek production by two-thirds, and around US$100 million to an interest reserve covering the first three semi-annual coupon payments. Remaining funds will advance Eskay Creek, cover transaction costs and bolster liquidity, while the company concurrently cancels an undrawn US$350 million term loan and cost over-run facility, moves that are intended to improve future operating margins, increase leverage to gold prices and enhance project economics for stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:SKE) stock is a Sell with a C$35.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Skeena Resources stock, see the TSE:SKE Stock Forecast page.

The score is primarily constrained by weak financial performance (pre-revenue profile, ongoing losses, and sustained cash burn with higher 2025 free-cash-flow pressure). Technicals add further downside bias (below key short-term averages with negative MACD), while valuation is also penalized by negative earnings and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on SKE stock, click here.

More about Skeena Resources

Skeena Resources Limited, branded as Skeena Gold & Silver, is a precious metals development company focused on advancing its fully permitted Eskay Creek gold-silver project in British Columbia’s Golden Triangle. The open-pit mine is under construction and targeted for initial production and cash flow in the second quarter of 2027, with expectations of being one of the world’s highest-grade, lowest-cost precious metals operations and a significant silver producer.

data.title: Eco Wave Power and Shell Conclude Port of Los Angeles Wave Energy Pilot With Final Report Submission
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Eco Wave Power Global ( (WAVE) ) has issued an announcement.

On March 31, 2026, Eco Wave Power Global announced it had submitted a final project completion report to Shell International Exploration and Production, formally closing their Port of Los Angeles wave energy pilot launched under a 2024 Pilot Test Agreement. The program, hosted at AltaSea, followed a structured path from U.S. coastline feasibility analysis to engineering design, installation and operational testing, with funding released in tranches upon milestone completion.

The pilot achieved full lifecycle execution, securing regulatory permits, demonstrating reliable operation in real marine conditions and integrating with port infrastructure without seabed anchoring, offshore construction or subsea cabling, all at a capital cost below $1 million. With environmental reviews finding no significant impact and a U.S. Department of Energy lab highlighting the advantages of coastal-structure-integrated wave systems, the Los Angeles site will continue as a demonstration and education facility, strengthening Eco Wave Power’s technical and regulatory credentials for future commercial-scale deployments on coastal infrastructure worldwide.

The most recent analyst rating on (WAVE) stock is a Hold with a $6.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Eco Wave Power Global stock, see the WAVE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on WAVE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, WAVE is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (unstable, shrinking revenue; ongoing losses; and worsening free cash flow burn). Technical indicators provide some near-term support with improving short-term momentum, but valuation is constrained by negative earnings and no dividend.

To see Spark’s full report on WAVE stock, click here.

More about Eco Wave Power Global

Eco Wave Power Global AB is a Nasdaq-listed onshore wave energy technology company that converts ocean and sea waves into electricity using patented systems installed on existing coastal infrastructure such as breakwaters, piers and jetties. The company targets clean, cost-efficient power generation near coastal cities, ports and data-center heavy industrial hubs, and is expanding a 404.7 MW global pipeline with projects planned in Portugal, Taiwan and India, alongside Israel’s first grid-connected wave energy station and a U.S. pilot in Los Angeles.

Cadeler A/S Sponsored ADR ( (CDLR) ) has issued an update.

Cadeler A/S reported on March 31, 2026 that BW Altor Pte. Ltd., a Singapore-based investment vehicle ultimately controlled by Andreas Sohmen-Pao, has increased its ownership stake in the offshore wind contractor. Following the completion of a private placement and the redelivery of lent shares used to settle that offering, BW Altor now holds 109,007,909 Cadeler shares, equal to 28.24% of the company’s share capital and voting rights.

The new position reflects both the return of 22,968,883 shares previously lent to facilitate delivery-versus-payment settlement for other investors and BW Altor’s purchase of additional shares on March 27, 2026. The rise from a prior 22.10% stake underscores a strengthened, long-term anchor shareholder position after Cadeler’s March 26, 2026 private placement of 35,095,758 new shares and the March 30, 2026 increase in share capital, developments that may bolster the company’s funding base for future offshore wind growth.

An update from ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B ( (AMUB) ) is now available.

On March 31, 2026, UBS Group AG and UBS AG reported the final outcomes of two rescission offers covering specific past debt securities transactions that ran from February 19 to March 20, 2026. These offers allowed certain investors to unwind previous purchases of UBS Group AG and UBS Americas Inc. notes under defined conditions.

For the UBS Group AG rescission offer, investors submitted valid claims on about $2.801 million in principal amount of securities, with settlement scheduled around April 2, 2026, indicating only a modest portion of eligible notes was returned. In contrast, no valid claims were submitted under the UBS Americas rescission offer, suggesting limited investor demand to unwind those positions and implying minimal impact on UBS’s overall funding profile and outstanding debt structure.

ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B ( (AMUB) ) has provided an update.

On March 31, 2026, UBS Group AG and UBS AG reported the final results of two rescission offers launched on February 19, 2026 and closed on March 20, 2026, aimed at certain purchasers of specified UBS Group and UBS Americas Inc. debt securities. The announcement details how many investors chose to unwind prior purchases and receive their money back, clarifying the scale of the liability for the bank.

Zhibao Technology Inc. Class A ( (ZBAO) ) has provided an announcement.

Zhibao Technology Inc., a Cayman Islands–registered InsurTech holding company, runs its main operations in China through subsidiaries Zhibao China and Sunshine Insurance Brokers, where it has pioneered a 2B2C digital embedded insurance model. By integrating customized insurance solutions into the online ecosystems of over 3,100 business and institutional partners, the company serves more than 27 million end customers with scenario‑specific brokerage and MGU services powered by its proprietary PaaS, big data and AI.

In a Form 6‑K filed on March 31, 2026, Zhibao reported unaudited results for the six months ended December 31, 2025, showing revenue rising 41% year‑on‑year to RMB 206.0 million and a swing from a small net loss to a modest net profit of RMB 0.6 million, underscoring improving operating performance. Over the same period, the company continued to fund growth via a convertible‑note arrangement with investor L1, settling part of the debt through share issuance and cash while leaving a small balance outstanding, and on September 30, 2025, acquired 51% of Zhonglian for RMB 25.5 million, a move that expands its footprint ahead of further 2B2C channel and technology‑driven expansion in China’s early‑stage embedded insurance market.

The most recent analyst rating on (ZBAO) stock is a Hold with a $0.76 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Zhibao Technology Inc. Class A stock, see the ZBAO Stock Forecast page.

More about Zhibao Technology Inc. Class A

Zhibao Technology Inc. is a Cayman Islands–incorporated holding company that operates a leading high‑growth InsurTech business in China through its PRC subsidiaries, including Zhibao China and Sunshine Insurance Brokers. It focuses on 2B2C digital embedded insurance, delivering customized insurance brokerage and managing general underwriter services via proprietary PaaS technology to over 3,100 business channels and more than 27 million end customers across sectors such as travel, sports, logistics, utilities and e‑commerce.

Under its model, Zhibao represents end customers as their authorized broker, partnering with more than 100 insurance companies to embed tailored products into the digital touchpoints of corporate and government clients. The company leverages big data and AI to iterate over 40 scenario‑specific digital insurance solutions, positioning itself as an early mover in China’s nascent embedded insurance brokerage market and aiming to expand both its business channel base and direct‑to‑consumer capabilities.

Global-e Online ( (GLBE) ) has shared an update.

Global-E Online clarified on March 31, 2026 that its recently filed 2025 annual report contained a scrivener’s error regarding the contribution of existing merchants to GMV. The company stated that existing merchants accounted for 87% and 91% of GMV in 2024 and 2025, respectively, instead of 87% and 87% as previously reported.

Global-E further noted that in 2025 new merchants generated $623 million of GMV out of a total $6.569 billion, confirming that new merchants represented 9% of GMV while existing merchants contributed 91%. The correction underscores the continuing dominance of the existing merchant base in driving the company’s transaction volumes and may reassure investors about the stability and loyalty of its core clientele.

Farmhouse, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors that it will delay its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The company is based in San Francisco and the late filing relates specifically to its annual report.

Management cites the need for additional time to complete preparation and review of its financial statements, including related disclosures and audit procedures. The delay is framed as an effort to avoid unreasonable effort or expense in meeting the original deadline.

Farmhouse, Inc. states that it expects to file the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) on or before April 15, 2026, which falls within the 15-day extension window allowed under SEC Rule 12b-25. Investors should therefore anticipate the annual results becoming publicly available within that grace period.

The company does not anticipate any significant change in results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year, according to the notification. No preliminary financial figures are provided, and the statements remain forward-looking until the full audited Form 10-K (Yearly Report) is filed.

Farmhouse, Inc. confirms that all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed on time, signaling ongoing compliance with SEC reporting rules. The notification is signed by Chief Executive Officer Evan Horowitz on March 31, 2026, underscoring executive responsibility for the delayed filing and the plan to complete it within the allowed extension.

The company has indicated that it does not anticipate any significant change in its results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year. Investors should remember that any expectations about timing or financial impact are forward-looking statements and could change as the company finalizes its numbers.

data.title: Professional Diversity Outlines Costly Pivot Toward Blockchain Growth
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Professional Diversity ( (IPDN) ).

Professional Diversity Network reported on March 31, 2026 that its fiscal 2025 revenue slipped 2.7% to about $6.55 million, as softer hiring demand and lower employer spending weighed on its TalentAlly and NAPW networks, while RemoteMore’s contracted software development revenue rose sharply. Despite cutting sales and marketing costs, the company’s net loss from continuing operations widened to roughly $6.51 million on higher costs and surging general and administrative expenses, as it raised more capital and committed nearly $9.5 million to copyright-related and digital token assets to reposition itself toward technology- and blockchain-enabled growth.

In the fourth quarter of 2025, TalentAlly and NAPW revenues fell double digits year on year, while RemoteMore posted more than 50% growth, but higher costs drove the quarterly net loss from continuing operations up 272% to about $2.9 million. The balance sheet showed total assets more than doubling to about $17.9 million, driven largely by long-term investments, against a sharp drop in cash, higher current liabilities and an increase in stockholders’ equity, underscoring a strategic but costly pivot that could pressure near-term results while aiming to enhance long-term positioning.

The most recent analyst rating on (IPDN) stock is a Hold with a $1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Professional Diversity stock, see the IPDN Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on IPDN Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, IPDN is a Neutral.
